body_txt: When a character tries to recount an anecdote or tell a story, usually related to the situation their friends are experiencing, they will get carried away with unnecessary exposition and go off in absurd and comedic tangents. By the time they have reached the ending, the intended moral or theme of the story is lost. The Cloudcuckoolander is often prone to this, as are talkative old men . May overlap with No Sense of Humor . Compare Metaphorgotten , Ice-Cream Koan , Disorganized Outline Speech . Also compare Fractured Fairy Tale , in which it's due to the author's deliberation, Big-Lipped Alligator Moment when the story basically has no significance to the plot, and Sidetracked by the Analogy , when it's the listener who takes the meaning of the story off its course. May overlap with Beat Without a "But" , when you expect the story to have some resolution or plot-turning event, but it doesn’t and just ends short. Contrast "Shaggy Dog" Story , where the story does go somewhere— it's just not impressive once it does.
